preferred.ja: コンピュートセッション
preferred.th: เซสชันการคำนวณ

concepts.description: The isolated compute environment where users run code, notebooks, or applications. Use "session" as shorthand after first full mention.

preferred.ja: インタラクティブセッション

concepts.description: Compute session type: User interacts after creation.
Compute session type: Pre-defined script execution.
Compute session type: Model serving sessions.

preferred.th: โฟลเดอร์จัดเก็บ

concepts.description: Persistent storage that survives session termination. Use "storage folder" in procedural text, "virtual folder" or "vfolder" in introductions.

preferred.th: โฟลเดอร์เสมือน

concepts.description: Technical-context term for the storage folder (virtual folder). Referred to as "vfolder" in technical contexts.
Created by individual users for personal use.
Created by administrators, shared across project members.

preferred.th: โดเมน

concepts.description: The top-level organizational unit for authority and resource control. Do NOT use "organization" or "affiliate" as substitutes.

preferred.th: โปรเจกต์

concepts.description: The work unit within a domain. Users can belong to multiple projects. Do NOT use "group" as a substitute for project.

preferred.th: กลุ่มทรัพยากร

concepts.description: A grouping of agents (compute nodes) with similar hardware specifications. Do NOT use "scaling group" (deprecated internal term).

preferred.th: คีย์แพร์

concepts.description: Authentication credentials for API access. Always "keypair" (one word, no hyphen). Never "key pair" or "key-pair".

preferred.ja: エージェント, エージェントノード
preferred.th: เอเจนต์, โหนดเอเจนต์

concepts.description: The compute node that runs containers (sessions). "Agent" for general reference, "agent node" when emphasizing the hardware aspect. Use "worker node" only in model serving context.

preferred.th: fGPU

concepts.description: Backend.AI's GPU virtualization technology that allows sharing a single physical GPU across multiple sessions. Numeric (abbreviated) form. Write "fractional GPU (fGPU)" on first mention, then "fGPU" thereafter.

preferred.th: GPU แบบเศษส่วน

concepts.description: Descriptive form of fGPU (Backend.AI's GPU virtualization technology).
The feature name (page title, menu item).

preferred.th: เรพลิกา

concepts.description: An individual running instance of a deployed model service. The desired/active count of these is managed by the deployment and auto-scaling rules. Documentation section: deployment/deployment.md

preferred.ja: 自動スケーリングルール
preferred.th: กฎการปรับขนาดอัตโนมัติ

concepts.description: The rule that automatically adjusts a deployment's replica count based on live metrics. Match the UI label exactly: KO uses "오토스케일링" (the autoScalingRule i18n keys), NOT "자동 스케일링". Documentation section: deployment/deployment.md

preferred.th: โหนดกายภาพ

concepts.description: The physical machine (or virtual machine) hosting session nodes. Used in the cluster-mode setup description to explain how a managed node and worker nodes are distributed across machines. The cluster-mode UI tooltips (DescSingleNode / DescMultiNode) use this term. Do NOT confuse with "agent node" — in the cluster-mode node-distribution context, use "physical node" to match the UI.
The access point created for a served model.
The operation of running predictions through a served model.

preferred.th: App Proxy

concepts.description: The standalone, per-resource-group proxy service that relays in-container application traffic directly between the user's browser and the compute session's Agent. Renamed from WSProxy to App Proxy in the UI in FR-2841. Use "App Proxy" only; do not reference the old "WSProxy" name in user-facing documentation. UI label stays in English across all locales.

preferred.ja: App Proxy サーバアドレス
preferred.th: ที่อยู่เซิร์ฟเวอร์ App Proxy

concepts.description: Resource Group settings field label for the App Proxy server address.

preferred.th: โทเค็น API ของ App Proxy

concepts.description: Resource Group settings field label for the App Proxy API token.
The runtime environment of a compute session. Do NOT use "container" and "kernel" interchangeably.
The base image (snapshot) from which containers are created. Do NOT use "container" and "kernel" interchangeably.

preferred.th: RBAC

concepts.description: Role-Based Access Control. Keep as abbreviation across all languages.

preferred.th: บทบาท

concepts.description: A named set of permissions assignable to users. The Korean i18n uses "권한" for both "Role" and "Permission" in different contexts.

preferred.th: สิทธิ์

concepts.description: A fine-grained access rule within a role. "세부 권한" is used specifically for fine-grained permissions within a role.

preferred.th: การมอบหมายบทบาท

concepts.description: The association of a user to a role.

preferred.th: ประเภทขอบเขต

concepts.description: The level at which a permission applies.

preferred.th: ประเภทสิทธิ์

concepts.description: The category of resource a permission controls.
Standard access.
Manages users and resources within a domain.
Full system access across all domains.
Sidebar menu item. Documentation section: summary/summary.md
Sidebar menu item. Documentation section: session_page/session_page.md

Sidebar menu item. Documentation section: statistics/statistics.md
Sidebar menu item. Documentation section: user_settings/user_settings.md

preferred.th: การจัดสรรแบบเป็นธรรม

concepts.description: The scheduling policy that balances resource-allocation fairness across users within a resource group. The KO UI keeps the English label "Fair Share" (webui.menu.FairShare).
The scheduler component that computes Fair Share priorities. Appears in Fair Share setting descriptions; no dedicated per-language UI label (documentation term).

preferred.th: ขีดจำกัดอัตรา

concepts.description: The keypair/credential setting capping API request frequency. The KO UI label is "최대 요청수" (credential.RateLimit).

preferred.th: แอ็กทีฟพูล

concepts.description: The set of model-service replicas currently receiving traffic through App Proxy. Membership tracks traffic and is independent of a replica's health status. See app-proxy.

preferred.th: คลังโมเดล

concepts.description: The Model Store feature/page for browsing and importing published models into a project.

preferred.th: นโยบายทรัพยากร

concepts.description: A named policy defining resource limits (CPU, memory, GPU, session count). WebUI has distinct keypair, user, and project resource policies. KO uses "자원 정책" (consistent with 자원 그룹).

avoid.reason: Reserve "worker node" for model serving context only
Renamed in FR-2841; `wsproxy` (code style) survives only as internal identifiers (DB columns, API route, constants), not a daemon
Use the Korean translation, not the transliteration, for consistency
Match the UI label; the autoScalingRule i18n keys use "오토스케일링"
Use the spaced form for consistency
Use the Korean translation, not the transliteration

verbs.context: Stop an in-flight or not-yet-committed operation (request, draft, running deployment rollout).
verbs.description: Use "Cancel" for stopping an action that has not yet had a permanent effect (a pending request, a model-service request, an unsaved form). Does not destroy persisted data. Maps to i18n `button.Cancel` / `modelService.Cancel`.

verbs.context: Permanently remove a standalone resource the user owns (storage folder, image, token, resource preset).
verbs.description: Use "Delete" for permanent removal of a resource that exists on its own. Maps to i18n `button.Delete`, `data.folders.Delete`, `environment.Delete`. NOTE: lexical only — whether a given Delete needs a typed-confirm modal is decided by destructive-confirmation.md from the action's reversibility, NOT from this word.

verbs.context: Detach an item from a collection / relationship without destroying the item itself (permission from a role, version from a list, member from a project).
verbs.description: Use "Remove" for taking an item out of a list or breaking an association — the underlying entity survives elsewhere. "Remove from list" is NOT "Delete permanently". Maps to i18n `rbac.RemovePermission`, `model.RemoveSelectedVersions`. Reserve "Delete" for destroying the standalone resource.

verbs.context: End a running compute session (interactive / batch / inference) or model-service endpoint.
verbs.description: Use "Terminate" for ending a running session/endpoint and releasing its resources. Maps to i18n `session.Terminate`, `session.TerminateSession`, `session.ForceTerminate`. Do not use "Stop" for this; "Stop" is reserved for pausable workloads.

verbs.context: Halt a process that can later be resumed or restarted (a service replica, a pausable job).
verbs.description: Use "Stop" only when the workload can be started again afterward. If the resources are released and the session ends, use "Terminate" instead.

verbs.context: Disable an account/credential/project/role so it can no longer be used, while keeping its data for later reactivation.
verbs.description: Approved verb is "Deactivate" (not "Inactivate"): the UI uses "Deactivate" 13x vs "Inactivate" 1x. Maps to i18n `credential.Deactivate`, `project.Deactivate`, `rbac.Deactivate`, `resourceGroup.Deactivate`. The single `usersettings.InactivateTheFollowingUsers` value is the drift this entry pins. Reversible: pair with Activate.

verbs.context: Permanently and irrecoverably erase an already-deactivated account/project/role and all its records.
verbs.description: Use "Purge" for the final, irreversible erase that follows "Deactivate". Distinct from "Delete": Purge implies the entity was first deactivated and now has its records expunged. Maps to i18n `project.Purge`, `project.PurgeProject`, `rbac.PurgeRole`.

verbs.context: Make an item invisible in the UI without deleting it (hide a column, hide a notice, hide a list entry).
verbs.description: Use "Hide" for purely cosmetic visibility changes that keep the underlying data intact and can be reversed by un-hiding. Never use "Hide" when data is actually removed.

verbs.context: Modify values of an existing entity that was created with initial values (resource group, user, role, preset, deployment, registry, resource policy).
verbs.description: Use "Edit" (KO: 수정) for actions that open or title a form modifying a previously created entity's values — modal titles, row actions, icon tooltips. Maps to i18n `button.Edit`, `credential.ModifyUserDetail` ("Edit User Detail"), `resourceGroup.ModifyResourceGroup` ("Edit Resource Group"). Do NOT use "Edit" for feature configuration — that stays "Settings" (KO: 설정, e.g., fair share settings) — nor for text/content editing where KO keeps 편집 (e.g., the vfolder file editor). The submit button of an edit form is "Save" (KO: 저장, i18n `button.Save`), not "Edit" or "Update".
